_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-38R Ondansetron (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template — ODT is MCA Optional.
level_display: Medication Reference — ODT Route MCA Optional
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-38R: Ondansetron — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
precautions: ODT route: patient must not be actively vomiting; tablet dissolves under tongue — ensure patient can cooperate
ODT administration is MCA OPTIONAL — only if MCA has authorized ODT route for EMS
Pediatric ODT: only for patients >30 kg
notes: Adult ODT (if not actively vomiting and >30 kg): 4 mg ODT one time; repeat dose, if needed, must be IV/IM only
Adult IV/IM (if actively vomiting or ODT unavailable): 4 mg; second dose 15 min later; total max 8 mg
Pediatric IV/IM: 0.1 mg/kg; maximum 4 mg; total max 8 mg
Pediatric ODT: 4 mg if >30 kg and not actively vomiting; total max 8 mg
ODT = MCA Optional — only authorized if MCA has approved ODT route

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — nausea/vomiting (ODT, not actively vomiting)
dosing.notes: ODT only if MCA has authorized route; patient must not be actively vomiting; only >30 kg. Any second dose must be IV/IM only and total dose including ODT must not exceed 8 mg

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — nausea/vomiting (actively vomiting or ODT unavailable)
dosing.dose: 4 mg IV/IM; may repeat 4 mg after 15 minutes; total maximum 8 mg
0.1 mg/kg IV/IM; maximum 4 mg per dose; total maximum 8 mg

dosing.population: Pediatric >30 kg — nausea/vomiting (ODT, not actively vomiting)
dosing.notes: ODT only if MCA has authorized route; only for patients >30 kg; not actively vomiting. Any second dose must be IV/IM only and total dose including ODT must not exceed 8 mg
